task: STARE AT A BLANK WALL FOR FIVE MINUTES.
subtitle: No phones. No music. No distractions. Just the void and your thoughts.

task: COUNT EVERY RIGHT ANGLE YOU CAN SEE.
subtitle: Don't estimate. Actually count. The number will surprise you.

task: LIST EVERY ADDRESS YOU HAVE EVER LIVED AT.
subtitle: In order. From the beginning. See how far back your memory actually goes.

task: DESCRIBE YOUR CHILDHOOD KITCHEN IN BRUTAL DETAIL.
subtitle: Tiles. Smells. Light through windows. What was on the fridge. Go.

task: SAY YOUR NAME OUT LOUD THIRTY TIMES.
subtitle: It will start sounding like someone else's. Trust the process.

task: FIND EVERY CIRCULAR OBJECT IN THIS ROOM.
subtitle: Coins count. Lids count. A glass rim. How many can you find?

task: LISTEN TO ONLY AMBIENT SOUND FOR FOUR MINUTES.
subtitle: No music. No speech. Just what's already happening. Notice the layers.

task: WRITE DOWN EVERY REGRET FROM LAST WEEK.
subtitle: Big ones and small ones. The petty counts. Get them out of your head.

task: IMAGINE YOUR PERFECT BREAKFAST AND DESCRIBE IT.
subtitle: Temperature. Texture. Plating. Lighting. Make it vivid. Make it hurt.

task: COUNT YOUR BREATHS UNTIL YOU REACH ONE HUNDRED.
subtitle: No hyperventilating. Natural pace. Your mind will wander. Bring it back.

task: FIND ALL THE BLUE THINGS IN YOUR IMMEDIATE SPACE.
subtitle: Light blue. Dark blue. Navy. Teal counts. Make a pile. Photograph it.

task: WRITE A LETTER TO YOURSELF FIVE YEARS AGO.
subtitle: One page. Advice, warning, or just hello. Seal it mentally.

task: TOUCH EVERY SURFACE IN ARM'S REACH AND RANK THEM.
subtitle: Smoothest to roughest. Warmest to coldest. Use all your senses.

task: RECALL THE EXACT SEQUENCE OF YOUR MORNING.
subtitle: Minute by minute. Where did you actually spend time? You don't remember.

task: CALCULATE HOW MANY HOURS YOU HAVE LEFT TODAY.
subtitle: Until bed. Until an arbitrary endpoint. What will you do with them?

task: CLOSE YOUR EYES AND MAP THIS ROOM MENTALLY.
subtitle: Doors. Windows. Furniture. Objects. Then open your eyes and check.

task: NAME EVERY PERSON YOU HAVE DISAPPOINTED.
subtitle: Write or think. Go back ten years if you can. This will ache.

task: FIND SOMETHING THAT MAKES A SOUND WHEN MOVED.
subtitle: Create the best possible percussion. Tap patterns. Build a rhythm.

task: DESCRIBE YOUR REFLECTION WITHOUT LOOKING AT IT.
subtitle: From memory. How accurate will you be? Then check.

task: LIST EVERY MEAL YOU REMEMBER EATING THIS YEAR.
subtitle: Chronologically if you can. Note where you were. How many do you recall?

task: STRETCH EVERY JOINT IN YOUR BODY SLOWLY.
subtitle: Fingers, wrists, shoulders, spine, hips, knees, ankles. Find where you're tight.

task: WRITE DOWN YOUR TOP FIVE FEARS IN DETAIL.
subtitle: Don't edit. Raw. Specific. What exactly are you terrified of?

task: COUNT THE WORDS YOU CAN SEE WITHOUT MOVING.
subtitle: Signs, labels, text on objects. Be methodical. Don't move.

task: IMAGINE YOUR FUNERAL AND WRITE THREE EULOGIES.
subtitle: From different people. Kind. Harsh. Honest. What will they actually say?

task: HOLD YOUR BREATH UNTIL YOU ABSOLUTELY MUST BREATHE.
subtitle: Time it. Don't fight the panic. Just observe it.

task: FIND ALL RECTANGLES IN YOUR FIELD OF VISION.
subtitle: Books, screens, frames, tiles, doors. How many are there really?

task: WRITE THE ANGRIEST TEXT MESSAGE YOU NEVER SENT.
subtitle: To anyone. No filter. Leave it unsent. Delete it after.

task: IDENTIFY EVERY SHADOW IN THE ROOM AND TRACK IT.
subtitle: They're moving. Watch for five minutes. Notice the subtlety.

task: RECONSTRUCT YOUR LAST ARGUMENT WORD FOR WORD.
subtitle: As close as you can. What was actually said versus what you remember?

task: FEEL YOUR PULSE AND COUNT THE BEATS FOR ONE MINUTE.
subtitle: At rest. Note the rhythm. Notice how you influence it just by thinking.

task: DESCRIBE EVERY SMELL IN THIS ROOM WITHOUT MOVING.
subtitle: Air. Objects. Decay. Cleaning products. Absence of smell. List them.

task: LIST TEN THINGS YOU HAVE NOT DONE YET.
subtitle: In this life. Big ones. Small ones. Write them down to make them real.

task: CLOSE YOUR EYES AND GUESS YOUR LOCATION.
subtitle: Based on sounds and temperature alone. Then open and see how close.

task: WRITE A NASTY REVIEW OF THIS ROOM.
subtitle: Yelp voice. One star. Everything that's wrong. Then write a five-star one.

task: FIND OBJECTS THAT REFLECT LIGHT AND ARRANGE THEM.
subtitle: Glass, mirrors, metal. Create the best arrangement. Notice the glints.

task: RECALL EVERY COMPLIMENT YOU HAVE EVER RECEIVED.
subtitle: Childhood through now. See if you actually believe any of them.

task: WALK LIKE A DIFFERENT ANIMAL FOR THREE MINUTES.
subtitle: Fully commit. Let your body change. Notice how your mind follows.

task: DESCRIBE THIS MOMENT IN BRUTAL HONESTY.
subtitle: How you feel. What you're avoiding. What's true right now.

task: COUNT EVERY ITEM IN ONE DRAWER OR CONTAINER.
subtitle: Exactly. No estimates. Know the precise count before you start.

task: WRITE WHAT YOU WANT CARVED ON YOUR TOMBSTONE.
subtitle: Short epitaph. Genuine. Not cute. What do you actually want remembered?

task: CHANGE POSITIONS EVERY THIRTY SECONDS FOR SIX MINUTES.
subtitle: Sitting, standing, lying down. Keep moving. Feel the restlessness.

task: LOCATE THE COLDEST OBJECT WITHIN ARM'S REACH.
subtitle: Touch it. Hold it. Let your hand adjust. Why does it feel that way?

task: WRITE YOUR BIGGEST LIE AND WHY YOU TOLD IT.
subtitle: The worst one. The reason. What happened after. Write the truth.

task: FIND EVERY TEXT-BASED OBJECT AND READ IT ALOUD.
subtitle: Product labels, instructions, warnings. Hear the mundane spoken.

task: REMEMBER YOUR WORST BIRTHDAY AND DESCRIBE IT.
subtitle: What happened. What didn't. How did you feel. Write it now.

task: POINT AT OBJECTS AND RATE THEM BY USEFULNESS.
subtitle: One through ten. Justify your scores. Why is that object more useful?

task: CLOSE YOUR EYES AND DESCRIBE THIS ROOM BACKWARD.
subtitle: From the farthest object back to where you are. Spatial memory test.

task: WRITE A LETTER TO YOUR YOUNGER SELF AT TEN.
subtitle: One page. What would you tell them to do differently? Or not?

task: MAKE THE STRANGEST SOUND YOU CAN WITH YOUR MOUTH.
subtitle: Hold it. Layer it. Repeat. What are the limits of your vocal range?

task: LIST EVERY PERSON YOU HAVE KISSED OR WANTED TO.
subtitle: Chronologically. One-sided crushes count. Recognize the pattern.

task: FIND THE WARMEST SPOT IN THIS ROOM AND SIT THERE.
subtitle: Move around. Check corners. What creates the warmth? Stay for five minutes.

task: WRITE YOUR LIFE STORY IN EXACTLY ONE PARAGRAPH.
subtitle: Birth to now. Condensed. What becomes important when space is limited?

task: COUNT EVERY LIGHT SOURCE YOU CAN SEE.
subtitle: Natural and artificial. Bright and subtle. Some are just reflections.

task: DESCRIBE WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE FOR YOU IN DETAIL.
subtitle: Not money. Not status. What does your actual success feel like?

task: HOLD A POSITION UNTIL YOUR MUSCLES ABSOLUTELY ACHE.
subtitle: Wall sit, plank, or standing on one leg. Push until you break.

task: FIND EVERY PATTERN IN THIS ROOM AND CATALOG IT.
subtitle: Stripes, dots, grids, repetition. Make a visual inventory.

task: WRITE THE MOST EMBARRASSING MOMENT OF YOUR LIFE.
subtitle: In detail. The heat. The witnesses. The memory you've been burying.

task: IDENTIFY YOUR MOST REPEATED THOUGHT PATTERN.
subtitle: Watch your mind. What loops? What's the unhelpful script you replay?

task: LOCATE EVERY EDGE YOU CAN REACH WITH YOUR HANDS.
subtitle: Trace them. Feel the transitions. How many boundaries are in this space?

task: WRITE A SCATHING CRITICISM OF YOURSELF HONESTLY.
subtitle: All the ways you're failing. No filters. Then argue against each point.

task: LISTEN TO ONLY YOUR OWN BREATHING FOR FOUR MINUTES.
subtitle: Loud. Quiet. Patterns. Can you make it stop without passing out?

task: RANK EVERY CLOTHING ITEM YOU ARE WEARING BY COMFORT.
subtitle: Most comfortable to least. What would you remove first?

task: WRITE DOWN YOUR DEFINITION OF HAPPINESS SPECIFICALLY.
subtitle: Not philosophy. Your own. What makes you actually feel alive?

task: FIND ALL STRAIGHT LINES IN YOUR VISUAL FIELD.
subtitle: Architectural and object-based. Are any actually perfectly straight?

task: RECALL A CONVERSATION YOU REGRET WORD FOR WORD.
subtitle: What you said. What they said. What you wish you'd said instead.

task: HOLD EVERY TEXTURED OBJECT AND CATALOG SENSATIONS.
subtitle: Smooth, rough, bumpy, soft. Find the extremes. Close your eyes.

task: WRITE WHAT YOU ACTUALLY THINK ABOUT MOST PEOPLE.
subtitle: Your real opinion. Unfiltered. The unfair judgments you make instantly.

task: COUNT EVERY CORNER IN THIS ROOM EXACTLY.
subtitle: Internal and external. Ceiling, walls, floor. How many are there?

task: DESCRIBE YOUR IDEAL DAY HOUR BY HOUR IN DETAIL.
subtitle: Morning to night. Specific times. What would make it perfect?

task: WALK IN SLOW MOTION FOR EXACTLY FIVE MINUTES.
subtitle: Exaggerated movement. Feel every muscle engage. This is meditation.

task: FIND EVERY OBJECT THAT MAKES NO SOUND WHEN MOVED.
subtitle: Soft items. Squishy things. Silence within reach.

task: WRITE YOUR OBITUARY AS IF YOU DIED TODAY.
subtitle: Full obituary. Publications, survivors, accomplishments. Is it accurate?

task: IDENTIFY THE OLDEST ITEM IN THIS ROOM AND RESEARCH IT.
subtitle: When was it made? How has it changed? What has it witnessed?

task: CLOSE YOUR EYES AND DESCRIBE YOUR LOCATION TO A STRANGER.
subtitle: Make them understand exactly where you are without saying where.

task: LIST EVERY STUPID THING YOU SAID THIS WEEK.
subtitle: In detail. The context. How awkward was it actually?

task: FIND SOMETHING YOU HAVE NOT LOOKED AT IN A YEAR.
subtitle: Really examine it. What was it doing just existing here?

task: WRITE A SCRIPT FOR YOUR PERFECT ARGUMENT WITH SOMEONE.
subtitle: How it goes. What you say. How they respond. How it resolves.

task: RANK YOUR BODY PARTS BY USEFULNESS RATIONALLY.
subtitle: Brain, hands, eyes, heart. Not attraction. Function.

task: FIND EVERY TRANSPARENT OBJECT AND LOOK THROUGH IT.
subtitle: Glass, plastic, water. How does it distort what's behind?

task: WRITE THE TRUTH ABOUT YOUR RELATIONSHIP WITH YOUR BODY.
subtitle: Honest. Brutal. The things you never say out loud.

task: IDENTIFY EVERY SEAM IN YOUR CLOTHING AND FEEL THEM.
subtitle: Tag, stitching, hem. Normally invisible. Now they're all you feel.

task: WRITE WHAT YOU WOULD DO WITH ONE PERFECT DAY.
subtitle: No constraints. Money, health, ability. What would you actually choose?

task: COUNT THE ITEMS ON YOUR PERSON IN DETAIL.
subtitle: Pockets, jewelry, underwear. Every single thing you're carrying.

task: LOCATE THE MOST FRAGILE OBJECT IN THIS ROOM.
subtitle: Pick it up carefully. Understand its vulnerability. Why do you care?

task: WRITE YOUR MOST UNPOPULAR OPINION LOUDLY.
subtitle: Something you truly believe but never voice. Say it. Hear it.

task: FIND ALL THE DUST YOU CAN SEE IN LIGHT BEAMS.
subtitle: Particles floating. Where does it all come from? You live here.

task: WRITE A LOVE LETTER TO SOMEONE WHO REJECTED YOU.
subtitle: Say what you never could. All of it. Then burn or delete it.

task: IDENTIFY EVERY ANGLE THAT IS NOT NINETY DEGREES.
subtitle: Slanted furniture, pitched ceilings, diagonal objects. Map the weirdness.

task: DESCRIBE YOUR CHILDHOOD BEDROOM WITH EYES CLOSED.
subtitle: Carpet color. Wall posters. Where you sat to hide. Smell.

task: STAND ON ONE FOOT UNTIL BALANCE BECOMES DIFFICULT.
subtitle: Don't move your arms. Feel the core engage. How long can you last?

task: LIST EVERY TIME SOMEONE HURT YOU INTENTIONALLY THIS YEAR.
subtitle: Write it down. Was it warranted? Are you still angry? Should you be?

task: FIND EVERY OBJECT THAT CASTS A DISTINCT SHADOW.
subtitle: Sharp and clear. Watch how it changes as light shifts.

task: WRITE THE WORST THING YOU HAVE EVER BELIEVED ABOUT YOURSELF.
subtitle: The deepest doubt. Then argue why it might not be true.

task: TOUCH YOUR FACE AND NOTE EVERY SENSATION DISTINCTLY.
subtitle: Temperature, texture, moisture. You never actually feel your own skin.

task: WRITE YOUR LIFE IN BULLET POINTS BY DECADE.
subtitle: Age zero to ten, ten to twenty. See the shape of it.

task: COUNT EVERY OBJECT WITHIN EXACTLY ONE ARM'S REACH.
subtitle: Specific radius. Don't estimate. Touch each one to be sure.

task: LOCATE THE SPOT IN THIS ROOM WITH BEST ACOUSTICS.
subtitle: Speak or hum. Move around. Where does sound feel best?

task: WRITE EVERYTHING YOU HATE ABOUT WHERE YOU LIVE.
subtitle: Detailed complaints. Small and large. Then one thing you like.

task: FIND EVERY REFLECTIVE SURFACE AND STUDY YOUR IMAGE.
subtitle: Distorted and clear. Which reflection do you prefer?

task: DESCRIBE YOURSELF TO AN ALIEN WITHOUT USING HUMAN TERMS.
subtitle: No bones, flesh, DNA. Just describe what you are functionally.

task: IDENTIFY EVERY THING IN THIS ROOM YOU ACTUALLY OWN.
subtitle: Paid for or received as a gift. How much did you actually acquire?

task: WRITE YOUR FULL LIFE STORY FROM YOUR LOWEST POINT.
subtitle: Start there. Work to now. Was the recovery real or just distraction?

task: LISTEN TO THE SPACE BETWEEN SOUNDS FOR FIVE MINUTES.
subtitle: Silence isn't empty. Hear the layers of almost-nothing.

task: FIND EVERY OBJECT COLDER THAN ROOM TEMPERATURE.
subtitle: Metal, tile, glass. Feel them all. Why are some naturally cold?

task: WRITE WHAT YOU SECRETLY BELIEVE ABOUT THE FUTURE.
subtitle: Hope or dread? What do you actually think happens to you?

task: MAP EVERY WALL CRACK OR IMPERFECTION YOU CAN SEE.
subtitle: Trace them mentally. Where do they converge? Is this building settling?

task: WRITE THE PERSON YOU WERE FIVE YEARS AGO IN DETAIL.
subtitle: Beliefs, hopes, fears. How much have you changed? Has any of it stuck?

task: FIND THE SMALLEST OBJECT YOU CAN FIND AND EXAMINE IT.
subtitle: Really look. Every detail. What's it actually made of?

task: CLOSE YOUR EYES AND CATALOG EVERY SOUND AROUND YOU.
subtitle: Machinery, traffic, breathing, settling. Identify each source.

task: WRITE YOUR ACTUAL NET WORTH AND HOW YOU FEEL ABOUT IT.
subtitle: Money, possessions, relationships, health. Add it up honestly.

task: RANK EVERY PERSON YOU KNOW BY ACTUAL IMPACT ON YOUR LIFE.
subtitle: Not loyalty. Real influence. Who changed you most?

task: FIND EVERY VERTICAL LINE AND QUESTION IF IT IS PLUMB.
subtitle: Doors, walls, furniture. Use your eyes. How many are actually straight?

task: WRITE THE MEAN THING YOU THOUGHT ABOUT YOUR BEST FRIEND.
subtitle: The criticism you never say. What does it reveal about you?

task: FIND THE WARMEST OBJECT IN REACH AND HOLD IT.
subtitle: Feel the heat. Does it cool as you hold it? Notice the transfer.

task: DESCRIBE YOUR FIRST REAL HEARTBREAK IN FULL DETAIL.
subtitle: When you knew. The ache. What happened after. Where are they now?

task: IDENTIFY EVERY ITEM HERE YOU HAVE NEVER TOUCHED.
subtitle: What's so untouched it might as well not exist?

task: WRITE ALL THE WAYS YOU ARE BETTER THAN YOU THINK.
subtitle: Honestly. No modesty. What are you actually good at?

task: COUNT YOUR HEARTBEATS FOR EXACTLY THREE MINUTES.
subtitle: Hand on chest or neck. Constant? Irregular? Track the pattern.

task: FIND EVERY OBJECT THAT COLLECTS DUST AND CATALOG IT.
subtitle: Horizontal surfaces win. Why do you keep dusty things?

task: WRITE YOUR MOST SHAMEFUL MEMORY WITH NO EDITING.
subtitle: What you don't tell anyone. What still makes you cringe.

task: LOCATE EVERY WINDOW AND NOTE WHAT YOU CAN SEE.
subtitle: Weather, people, buildings, sky. What world exists outside right now?

task: WRITE HOW YOU ACTUALLY SPEND YOUR LEISURE TIME.
subtitle: Not what you wish. What you actually do. Be specific.

task: FIND EVERY ELECTRICAL OUTLET AND COUNT THEM EXACTLY.
subtitle: Each socket. Double outlets count as two. How many does this room have?

task: DESCRIBE YOUR RELATIONSHIP WITH SLEEP IN BRUTAL TERMS.
subtitle: How much? Quality? Dreams? Nightmares? When did it break?

task: TOUCH YOUR HAIR AND CATALOG ITS ACTUAL CONDITION.
subtitle: Texture, dryness, damage. What's it actually telling you?

task: WRITE THE ADVICE YOU WOULD GIVE YOUR YOUNGER SELF.
subtitle: The one thing. If you could tell them one truth. What is it?

task: FIND EVERY DOOR AND WINDOW IN THIS SPACE.
subtitle: Count them. Know exactly how you could leave right now.

task: WRITE EVERYTHING YOU WANT THAT YOU THINK IS IMPOSSIBLE.
subtitle: Dream big without restriction. Why do you think it's impossible?

task: IDENTIFY THE MOST USELESS OBJECT IN THIS ROOM AND EXPLAIN.
subtitle: Why is it still here? When is it ever actually used?

task: LISTEN FOR PATTERNS IN AMBIENT SOUND FOR FIVE MINUTES.
subtitle: Rhythm, repetition, cycles. Everything has a pulse.

task: WRITE YOUR DEEPEST INSECURITY IN CLINICAL TERMS.
subtitle: Emotionally describe the fear. What specifically terrifies you?

task: FIND EVERY THING HERE THAT SMELLS STRONG AND RANK THEM.
subtitle: Pleasant to putrid. Where do the strongest scents live?

task: DESCRIBE YOUR PHYSICAL PAIN OR DISCOMFORT IN DETAIL.
subtitle: All of it. Aches, twinges, tensions. Where is your body suffering?

task: LOCATE THE EXACT CENTER POINT OF THIS ROOM.
subtitle: Stand there. Feel the balance. Is it really the middle?

task: WRITE YOUR TOP THREE REGRETS AND WHY THEY STILL STING.
subtitle: Life choices. Words spoken. Actions taken or not taken.

task: FIND EVERY SOFT OBJECT AND ARRANGE THEM BY TEXTURE.
subtitle: Pillows, fabric, skin. Order from softest to roughest soft.

task: WRITE WHAT YOU WOULD SAY AT YOUR OWN FUNERAL.
subtitle: If you could speak from the dead. One final message.

task: COUNT EVERY SHADE OF COLOR IN THIS ROOM DISTINCTLY.
subtitle: Not just red. Crimson, burgundy, rust. How many distinct colors exist here?

task: BREATHE ONLY THROUGH YOUR NOSE FOR TEN MINUTES.
subtitle: No mouth breathing. Feel the resistance. Feel alive.

task: DESCRIBE YOUR CURRENT MENTAL STATE WITH BRUTAL HONESTY.
subtitle: How are you actually right now? Not how you're supposed to be.

task: FIND EVERY OBJECT THAT REFLECTS YOUR FACE AND STUDY IT.
subtitle: See yourself in metal, glass, water. Which reflection is truest?

task: WRITE A DETAILED COMPLAINT ABOUT YOUR LIFE RIGHT NOW.
subtitle: Everything you're frustrated with. Don't hold back.

task: IDENTIFY EVERY CEILING FIXTURE OR IMPERFECTION YOU SEE.
subtitle: Stains, cracks, marks. You never look up. Look now.

task: WRITE ABOUT THE LAST TIME YOU FELT TRULY ALIVE.
subtitle: When was it? What happened? How long ago was that?

task: FIND THE OBJECT WITH THE MOST COMPLICATED HISTORY HERE.
subtitle: How did you get it? What's its story? Why do you keep it?

task: CLOSE YOUR EYES AND SENSE YOUR BODY'S EXACT POSITION.
subtitle: Every limb. Every tension point. Can you feel yourself in space?

task: WRITE YOUR MOST UNPOPULAR TAKE ON ANOTHER PERSON.
subtitle: What do you genuinely think they don't want to hear?

task: FIND EVERY OBJECT YOU HAVE BROKEN AND NEVER MENTIONED.
subtitle: What still exists broken? Why didn't you tell anyone?

task: WRITE YOUR VERSION OF PERFECT HEALTH EXPLICITLY.
subtitle: Physically and mentally. What would need to be true?

task: LOCATE EVERY ITEM YOU USE LESS THAN ONCE A MONTH.
subtitle: Why do you still have it? When will you finally use or remove it?

task: LISTEN TO YOUR BODY'S INTERNAL SOUNDS FOR FOUR MINUTES.
subtitle: Heartbeat, breathing, digestion. You're a noisy animal inside.

task: WRITE YOUR HONEST THOUGHTS ABOUT GETTING OLDER.
subtitle: Fear, acceptance, dread. What does aging actually mean to you?

task: FIND EVERY OBJECT YOU HAVE HIDDEN OR KEPT SECRET.
subtitle: Why is it hidden? What would happen if someone found it?

task: STARE AT ONE SPOT ON THE WALL FOR FIVE MINUTES.
subtitle: Don't blink more than necessary. Let your perception shift.

task: WRITE THE ONE THING YOU WANT MOST IN LIFE SPECIFICALLY.
subtitle: Not vague. Actual. Detailed. Why do you want it?

task: CATALOG EVERY PIECE OF FURNITURE AND RATE ITS COMFORT.
subtitle: Sit in each one. Rank by actual comfort, not aesthetics.

task: TRACE YOUR VEINS WITH YOUR FINGER AND FOLLOW THEM.
subtitle: Blue roads under your skin. Where do they go? How many can you see?

task: WRITE ABOUT THE PERSON YOU MISS MOST RIGHT NOW.
subtitle: Where are they? Why do you miss them? Will you tell them?

task: FIND EVERY OBJECT THAT COULD HURT YOU IF THROWN.
subtitle: Sharp, heavy, hard. How many weapons exist in this space?

task: WRITE YOUR MOST CONTROVERSIAL BELIEF WITHOUT FILTER.
subtitle: The thing you think but never say. Who would hate you for it?

task: IDENTIFY EVERY SCENT MARKER IN THIS ROOM AND DESCRIBE IT.
subtitle: Coffee, perfume, must, decay. What does this room smell like?

task: HOLD EACH OBJECT AND GUESS ITS EXACT WEIGHT.
subtitle: Then look it up if you can. How wrong are you usually?

task: WRITE YOUR DEFINITION OF FRIENDSHIP AND ASSESS YOUR OWN.
subtitle: Do you have actual friends or just people who tolerate you?

task: FIND EVERY ITEM WITH TEXT AND READ IT ALL ALOUD.
subtitle: Every word. Every label. Hear the mundane spoken back to you.

task: DESCRIBE YOUR RELATIONSHIP WITH ANGER IN DETAIL.
subtitle: When do you feel it? How do you handle it? Is it healthy?

task: LOCATE THE ITEM YOU HAVE OWNED THE LONGEST.
subtitle: How old is it? How has it survived? Why haven't you discarded it?

task: CLOSE YOUR EYES AND WALK SLOWLY THROUGH THIS SPACE.
subtitle: Navigate by memory and instinct. Feel your way.

task: WRITE YOUR HONEST ASSESSMENT OF YOUR OWN CHARACTER.
subtitle: Good and bad. Are you the person you thought you'd be?

task: FIND EVERY THING THAT COULD BE CONSIDERED TRASH.
subtitle: What should you have thrown away already?

task: WRITE A BRUTAL HONEST REVIEW OF A PERSON YOU KNOW.
subtitle: One star. Everything that's wrong. Don't hold back.

task: SMELL EVERY OBJECT IN THIS ROOM IF POSSIBLE.
subtitle: What stories do scents tell? Some smell like their purpose.

task: WRITE ABOUT THE LAST ARGUMENT YOU HAD AND WHO LOST.
subtitle: Who was actually right? Do they know they won or lost?

task: FIND THE MOST VALUABLE OBJECT IN THIS ROOM BY MARKET VALUE.
subtitle: What would this room sell for? Does value equal meaning to you?

task: DESCRIBE YOUR RELATIONSHIP WITH TIME HONESTLY.
subtitle: Does it move fast? Slow? Do you waste it? Fear it?

task: FIND EVERY OBJECT THAT COULD HIDE ANOTHER OBJECT INSIDE.
subtitle: Boxes, containers, hollow spaces. What secrets could exist here?

task: WRITE ABOUT YOUR RELATIONSHIP WITH HAPPINESS SPECIFICALLY.
subtitle: Do you deserve it? Can you achieve it? Is it real?

task: MAKE A SOUND YOU HAVE NEVER MADE WITH YOUR VOICE.
subtitle: Weird, foreign, impossible. Expand your vocal range.

task: WRITE YOUR ACTUAL THOUGHTS ABOUT YOUR APPEARANCE.
subtitle: Honest. Not filtered. What do you actually think you look like?

task: FIND EVERY OBJECT THAT BELONGS SOMEWHERE ELSE.
subtitle: Out of place. Borrowed. In the wrong room. How much clutter is chaos?

task: WRITE YOUR EARLIEST MEMORY AND VERIFY IF IT'S REAL.
subtitle: How far back can you go? Is it actually your memory or a story?

task: IDENTIFY EVERY SEAM, JOINT, AND INTERSECTION IN WALLS.
subtitle: Where does this room fall apart? What's holding it together?

task: DESCRIBE YOUR ACTUAL RELATIONSHIP WITH MONEY.
subtitle: Fear, security, freedom. What does money really mean to you?

task: FIND EVERY OBJECT YOU WOULD GRAB IN AN EMERGENCY.
subtitle: What matters enough to save? What would you leave?

task: WRITE THE TRUTH ABOUT YOUR CHILDHOOD THAT HURTS.
subtitle: Something you don't talk about. Something that shaped you.

task: LISTEN TO YOUR ENVIRONMENT FOR CHANGES FOR FIVE MINUTES.
subtitle: Notice when sounds start and stop. What's the rhythm?

task: WRITE ABOUT SOMEONE YOU ENVIED AND WHY SPECIFICALLY.
subtitle: What did they have that you wanted? Do you still want it?

task: FIND EVERY OBJECT THAT HAS CHANGED COLOR OVER TIME.
subtitle: Faded, darkened, discolored. What causes change?

task: DESCRIBE YOUR RELATIONSHIP WITH LONELINESS IN DETAIL.
subtitle: Are you actually alone right now? Or just lonely? Different things.

task: IDENTIFY EVERY OBJECT REFLECTING LIGHT BACK TO LIGHT SOURCE.
subtitle: Mirrors, glass, shine. Follow the beams with your eyes.

task: WRITE WHAT YOU WOULD DO WITH ONE EXTRA YEAR OF LIFE.
subtitle: Specific plans. No vague dreams. What actually matters to you?

task: FIND EVERY OBJECT WITH A SERIAL NUMBER OR IDENTIFIER.
subtitle: Each thing is trackable. You own numbered things. Feel the specificity.

task: WRITE YOUR MOST AUTHENTIC SELF WITHOUT PERFORMANCE.
subtitle: Who are you when no one's watching? Is that who you actually are?

task: CLOSE YOUR EYES AND SENSE TEMPERATURE CHANGES IN ROOM.
subtitle: Warm spots, cold spots. What causes the variation?

task: WRITE ABOUT THE LAST TIME YOU CRIED AND WHY.
subtitle: Recent or long ago? What breaks you? Do you let it anymore?

task: FIND EVERY OBJECT WITH WEAR MARKS OR DAMAGE.
subtitle: What gets damaged by use? What's earned its scars?

task: DESCRIBE YOUR ACTUAL FEARS WITHOUT MINIMIZING THEM.
subtitle: Big ones. Small ones. What keeps you up at night?

task: LOCATE THE AREA WITH MOST LIGHT AND STUDY IT CLOSELY.
subtitle: Where does illumination concentrate? Is it natural or artificial?

task: WRITE THE TRUTH ABOUT YOUR GREATEST INSECURITY FULLY.
subtitle: No euphemisms. What makes you feel small? Own it.

task: FIND EVERY OBJECT YOU HAVE NEVER CONSIDERED BEFORE.
subtitle: Background items. Invisible things. Notice them now.

task: WRITE YOUR VERSION OF SUCCESS SPECIFICALLY AND HONESTLY.
subtitle: Not society's. Not parents'. Yours. What would winning feel like?

task: IDENTIFY EVERY CORNER OF THIS ROOM YOU CANNOT SEE.
subtitle: Blind spots. What exists outside your line of sight?

task: WRITE ABOUT THE RELATIONSHIP THAT HURT YOU MOST.
subtitle: What happened. Why it mattered. Are you still aching?

task: FIND EVERY OBJECT THAT COULD PROVIDE SHELTER OR COVER.
subtitle: Where would you hide in this room? What makes you feel safe?

task: DESCRIBE YOUR ACTUAL DREAMS AND NIGHTMARES VIVIDLY.
subtitle: Recurring ones. Symbolic meaning. What does your subconscious want?

task: FIND EVERY OBJECT THAT MAKES A SOUND WHEN STEPPED ON.
subtitle: Creak, crunch, snap. What gives way under your weight?

task: WRITE YOUR HONEST VIEW OF YOUR FAMILY RIGHT NOW.
subtitle: Love and resentment mixed. What do you actually feel?

task: LOCATE EVERY THIN WALL OR BARRIER YOU CAN SENSE.
subtitle: How much privacy do you really have? Can others hear you?

task: WRITE ABOUT WHAT YOU HATE MOST ABOUT OTHER PEOPLE.
subtitle: Their behaviors, attitudes, habits. Be specific and harsh.

task: FIND EVERY OBJECT THAT COULD BE USED AS A WEAPON.
subtitle: Potential violence exists in every room. Know what's available.

task: WRITE WHAT YOU BELIEVE ABOUT YOUR OWN MORTALITY.
subtitle: When? How? Fear or acceptance? What does death mean?

task: IDENTIFY EVERY TRANSITION OR THRESHOLD IN THIS SPACE.
subtitle: Doorways, steps, level changes. Where does one area become another?

task: WRITE YOUR MOST RADICAL OPINION ON MODERN SOCIETY.
subtitle: What's fundamentally broken? What would you change?

task: FIND EVERY OBJECT THAT COLLECTS MOISTURE OR LIQUID.
subtitle: Where does water gather? What's damp or sticky?

task: DESCRIBE YOUR RELATIONSHIP WITH GUILT IN FULL DETAIL.
subtitle: When do you feel it? Is it warranted? How do you handle it?

task: LOCATE THE ITEM THAT TOOK THE MOST TIME TO ACQUIRE.
subtitle: What was hard to get? Was it worth the effort?

task: WRITE YOUR IDEAL RELATIONSHIP CHARACTERISTICS EXPLICITLY.
subtitle: What do you actually need? Don't romanticize. Be realistic.

task: FIND EVERY OBJECT WITH WRITING ON IT AND DOCUMENT IT.
subtitle: Names, dates, messages. What do people write on things?

task: WRITE YOUR ACTUAL STANCE ON FORGIVENESS AND REDEMPTION.
subtitle: Can people change? Should they be forgiven? What's your limit?

task: IDENTIFY THE OLDEST MARK OR STAIN IN THIS ROOM.
subtitle: How long has it been there? What caused it? Why hasn't it been cleaned?

task: WRITE ABOUT YOUR ACTUAL RELATIONSHIP WITH SPIRITUALITY.
subtitle: Belief, doubt, searching. What do you actually believe?

task: FIND EVERY OBJECT MADE FROM NATURAL MATERIALS.
subtitle: Wood, stone, cloth, leather. What's authentic versus synthetic?

task: DESCRIBE YOUR ACTUAL AMBITIONS WITHOUT SHAME.
subtitle: What do you actually want to achieve? Not what you should want.

task: LOCATE EVERY SURFACE THAT COLLECTS CLUTTER OR DEBRIS.
subtitle: Horizontal surfaces. Why do they accumulate stuff?

task: WRITE YOUR MOST HONEST ASSESSMENT OF YOUR MENTAL HEALTH.
subtitle: Without diagnosis or therapy speak. How are you actually doing?

task: FIND EVERY OBJECT THAT COULD PLAY A SOUND IF USED.
subtitle: Click, snap, buzz, hum. Build a percussion set from mundane items.

task: WRITE WHAT YOU WOULD TELL YOUR FUTURE SELF TO CHANGE.
subtitle: One year from now. What do you want to have done differently?

task: IDENTIFY EVERY WASTED SPACE OR EMPTY AREA HERE.
subtitle: Dead corners. Unused areas. What could be better utilized?

task: WRITE ABOUT YOUR ACTUAL RELATIONSHIP WITH WORK AND PURPOSE.
subtitle: Is what you do meaningful? Would you do it without money?

task: FIND EVERY OBJECT THAT COULD CATCH FIRE EASILY.
subtitle: Paper, cloth, dry materials. What's most flammable here?

task: WRITE YOUR DEFINITION OF LOVE WITHOUT SENTIMENT.
subtitle: Practically. What does it actually mean? How do you know when it's real?

task: LOCATE THE AREA WITH MOST SHADOW AND ANALYZE IT.
subtitle: Darkness. Blind spots. What can't be seen?

task: WRITE WHAT YOU ACTUALLY THINK ABOUT ONLINE IDENTITY.
subtitle: Is it you? A version of you? A complete lie? Who's the real you?

task: FIND EVERY OBJECT THAT COULD PROVIDE LIGHT OR VISIBILITY.
subtitle: Lamps, candles, reflectors. How many ways can you illuminate?

task: WRITE ABOUT YOUR ACTUAL RELATIONSHIP WITH PAIN.
subtitle: Physical or emotional. How much can you tolerate? Should you?

task: IDENTIFY EVERY OBJECT THAT SMELLS LIKE NOTHING AT ALL.
subtitle: The inodorous things. Does absence of smell mean clean?

task: WRITE YOUR MOST HONEST EVALUATION OF YOUR INTELLIGENCE.
subtitle: How smart are you really? In what ways? What are your limits?

task: FIND EVERY OBJECT THAT COULD HOLD LIQUID IF NEEDED.
subtitle: Containers, cups, jars. What's available to you?

task: WRITE ABOUT YOUR ACTUAL RELATIONSHIP WITH CONTROL.
subtitle: Do you need it? Fear losing it? Give it away?

task: LOCATE THE ITEM MOST VALUABLE EMOTIONALLY TO YOU.
subtitle: Worth nothing on a market. Worth everything to you. Why?

task: WRITE WHAT YOU BELIEVE HAPPENS AFTER DEATH TRULY.
subtitle: Not religious doctrine. What do you actually think?

task: FIND EVERY OBJECT WITH A MANUFACTURER'S MARK OR LABEL.
subtitle: Who made your stuff? Brands and origins everywhere.

task: WRITE YOUR ACTUAL VIEW OF PRIVILEGE AND UNFAIRNESS.
subtitle: How lucky are you? What's unearned? What did you actually achieve?

task: IDENTIFY EVERY COLOR THAT REPEATS MORE THAN TWICE HERE.
subtitle: Dominant hues. What did someone decide this room should be?

task: WRITE YOUR HONEST THOUGHTS ABOUT AUTHENTICITY VERSUS PERFORMANCE.
subtitle: Are they ever the same? Can you be genuine in public?

task: FIND EVERY OBJECT THAT REQUIRES MAINTENANCE TO FUNCTION.
subtitle: What's high-maintenance in your life? Is it worth the effort?

task: WRITE ABOUT YOUR ACTUAL RELATIONSHIP WITH YOUR MORTALITY.
subtitle: Do you accept it? Deny it? Fear it? Does it drive you?

task: LOCATE EVERY AREA THAT SHOWS WEAR OR AGE VISIBLY.
subtitle: Decay. Deterioration. What breaks down first?

task: WRITE WHAT YOU ACTUALLY OWE THE WORLD AND TO WHOM.
subtitle: Debts. Responsibilities. Can you ever repay?

task: FIND EVERY OBJECT THAT SERVES NO PRACTICAL PURPOSE HERE.
subtitle: Decoration, art, sentiment. Why do you keep things that don't work?

task: WRITE YOUR FINAL TRUTH ABOUT BEING ALIVE RIGHT NOW.
subtitle: This moment. This space. This body. What's the actual reality?

task: ASK THE NEAREST PERSON WHAT THEIR BEST MEAL WAS THIS WEEK.
subtitle: Not their favourite meal ever. Just this week. It's a better question than it sounds.

task: COUNT HOW MANY TIMES SOMEONE TOUCHES THEIR PHONE IN TWO MINUTES.
subtitle: Don't tell them you're counting. Just watch. Then report back.

task: TAKE TURNS DESCRIBING EACH OTHER USING ONLY BUILDING MATERIALS.
subtitle: Brick? Glass? Steel? Concrete? No explaining your answer.

task: ASK SOMEONE WHAT THEY WERE DOING AT EXACTLY NOON TODAY.
subtitle: Not what they planned to be doing. What they actually were doing. People rarely remember.

task: STUDY SOMEONE'S HANDS FOR ONE MINUTE WITHOUT THEM NOTICING.
subtitle: Calluses? Jewellery? Nail polish? Scars? Then describe one detail back to them.

task: FIND FIVE THINGS YOU BOTH HAVE NEVER DONE.
subtitle: Talk until you find five experiences neither of you has had. Doesn't matter how ordinary they are.

task: RACE TO NOTICE WHEN SOMEONE FIRST BLINKS AFTER YOU POINT IT OUT.
subtitle: Once they're aware of their own blinking, it becomes automatic again. Who notices first?

task: ASK WHAT ITEM IN THIS ROOM REMINDS THEM OF SOMEONE THEY KNOW.
subtitle: Listen to why. The answer is always more interesting than the object.

task: DESCRIBE YOUR DAY USING ONLY QUESTIONS.
subtitle: They have to guess what happened from your questions alone.

task: COMPETE TO NAME A SONG WITH THE WORD FRIDAY IN IT.
subtitle: First person to think of one wins. Doesn't have to be the obvious one.

task: WATCH SOMEONE'S FACE WHILE THEY READ SOMETHING ON THEIR PHONE.
subtitle: Notice the micro-expressions. Then ask them what they just read. Compare your reading to theirs.

task: ASK THEM TO TEACH YOU SOMETHING THEY'RE SURPRISINGLY GOOD AT.
subtitle: Everyone has one. Sit for three minutes and let them show you.

task: NAME THREE SMELLS YOU BOTH HATE WITHOUT SAYING THEM OUT LOUD.
subtitle: Act them out or describe them badly until the other person guesses.

task: OBSERVE HOW MANY TIMES SOMEONE CROSSES THEIR LEGS IN FIVE MINUTES.
subtitle: People fidget. This is the fidget baseline.

task: ASK WHAT THEY WOULD DO WITH ONE EXTRA HOUR TOMORROW.
subtitle: The answer usually reveals what they're actually wanting to do but aren't.

task: TAKE TURNS MAKING THE WORST IMPRESSION OF THE OTHER PERSON.
subtitle: No holding back. Nail the voice, the walk, the specific habit.

task: COUNT THE NUMBER OF TIMES SOMEONE SAYS THEIR FAVOURITE WORD.
subtitle: Ask them what they think it is first. Usually they're wrong.

task: DESCRIBE YOUR IDEAL TUESDAY WITHOUT MENTIONING WHAT YOU'D DO.
subtitle: Only describe the feeling, the weather, the sounds, the pace.

task: FIND ONE THING YOU BOTH REMEMBER DIFFERENTLY ABOUT LAST MONTH.
subtitle: Two people. One event. Two conflicting memories. Who's right?

task: RACE TO SPOT SOMEONE CHECKING THE TIME IN THIS ROOM.
subtitle: Who catches someone looking at their watch or phone first?

task: ASK WHAT THEY THINK THEY'RE WORSE AT THAN MOST PEOPLE.
subtitle: Listen without defending them or listing things they're good at.

task: TAKE TURNS NAMING THINGS THAT ARE DEFINITELY OVERRATED.
subtitle: Be specific. Not 'pizza'. 'Truffle oil on pizza'.

task: WATCH HOW MANY TIMES SOMEONE ADJUSTS THEIR CLOTHING IN EIGHT MINUTES.
subtitle: Pulling down a shirt. Retying shoelaces. Smoothing hair. Count them all.

task: ASK THEM WHAT QUESTION THEY'VE ALWAYS WANTED TO ASK YOU.
subtitle: They have one. Maybe two. Let them ask it.

task: DESCRIBE A PLACE YOU BOTH KNOW USING ONLY COLOURS.
subtitle: No names. Just paint a picture with shades and hues.

task: SPOT WHO IN THIS ROOM LOOKS MOST WORRIED RIGHT NOW.
subtitle: Not angry. Not sad. Specifically worried. What gave it away?

task: ASK WHAT THEY'RE AVOIDING THINKING ABOUT RIGHT NOW.
subtitle: They'll know instantly. Listen. Don't try to solve it.

task: TAKE TURNS COMPLETING THIS SENTENCE: I'M GOOD AT BEING IGNORED.
subtitle: Each of you explains in two sentences. No more.

task: COUNT HOW MANY TIMES SOMEONE MAKES EYE CONTACT WITH YOU IN ONE MINUTE.
subtitle: Most people look away more than they look at you.

task: ASK THEM TO DESCRIBE YOU USING ONLY OBJECTS, NO ADJECTIVES.
subtitle: A clock? A key? A broken umbrella? Don't explain.

task: PLAY MONEY: THEY PICK BETWEEN TWO AWFUL THINGS YOU NAME.
subtitle: Would you rather eat a sock or a shoe? Keep going until someone laughs.

task: OBSERVE SOMEONE'S HAIRLINE FOR ONE MINUTE STRAIGHT.
subtitle: How many individual hairs do you think you counted? Compare notes.

task: ASK WHAT THEY WERE AFRAID OF AS A CHILD THAT STILL HAUNTS THEM.
subtitle: Some fears never really leave. This one probably hasn't.

task: NAME SOMETHING EVERYONE PRETENDS TO LIKE BUT SECRETLY DOESN'T.
subtitle: Cilantro? Networking? Waking up early? Go deeper.

task: WATCH SOMEONE'S SHOULDERS WHILE THEY TALK FOR EXACTLY TWO MINUTES.
subtitle: Do they rise when they're excited? Tense? Shrug? Catalogue it.

task: ASK THEM THE MOST CONFUSING TEXT MESSAGE THEY'VE EVER RECEIVED.
subtitle: Let them read it to you if they still have it. Decode it together.

task: TAKE TURNS NAMING SOUNDS THAT MAKE YOU BOTH DEEPLY UNCOMFORTABLE.
subtitle: Not scary. Uncomfortable. Nails on chalkboard territory.

task: SPOT THE PERSON IN THIS ROOM WITH THE BEST POSTURE.
subtitle: Then adopt their exact position for one minute. How does it feel?

task: ASK WHAT THEY KNOW ABOUT YOU THAT YOU DON'T REALIZE THEY KNOW.
subtitle: They've observed something. Ask them to tell you.

task: DESCRIBE YOUR WORST MORNING USING ONLY VERBS IN PAST TENSE.
highlight: USING ONLY VERBS IN PAST TENSE
subtitle: Woke. Stumbled. Burned. Spilled. See how vivid you can make it.

task: COUNT HOW MANY TIMES SOMEONE LAUGHS, GENUINELY, IN TEN MINUTES.
subtitle: Track only the real ones. Not the polite laughs.

task: ASK WHAT THEY SPEND THE MOST TIME THINKING ABOUT THAT'S STUPID.
subtitle: Everyone has something they know they waste mental energy on. Name yours.

task: TAKE TURNS ACTING OUT THE MOST DRAMATIC VERSION OF A SNEEZE.
subtitle: Go overboard. Commit. Make it absurd.

task: WATCH HOW SOMEONE HOLDS THEIR CUP OR WATER BOTTLE FOR FIVE MINUTES.
subtitle: Tight grip? Loose? Do they switch hands? Notice the pattern.

task: ASK WHAT COMPLIMENT THEY'VE RECEIVED THAT ACTUALLY STUCK WITH THEM.
subtitle: Not generic praise. Something specific that landed.

task: NAME FIVE THINGS THAT COST WAY MORE THAN THEY SHOULD.
highlight: COST WAY MORE THAN THEY SHOULD
subtitle: Coffee? Airport food? Software? Specificity counts.

task: SPOT SOMEONE DOING SOMETHING WITHOUT REALIZING THEY'RE DOING IT.
subtitle: Then gently point it out and watch their reaction.

task: ASK THEM WHAT THEY WERE DOING AT THIS EXACT TIME LAST WEEK.
subtitle: Not what they were planning to do. What they actually were doing.

task: TAKE TURNS FINISHING THIS: NOBODY REALIZES HOW MUCH I HATE.
highlight: NOBODY REALIZES HOW MUCH I HATE
subtitle: Something petty. Something you've never said out loud.

task: COUNT HOW MANY TIMES SOMEONE'S EYES LOOK DOWN IN THREE MINUTES.
subtitle: Shame? Shyness? Thinking? What does the pattern suggest?

task: ASK WHAT FOOD THEY COULD GENUINELY NEVER GET TIRED OF.
subtitle: They'll have one. Listen to why it's different from their other favourites.

task: DESCRIBE SOMEONE YOU BOTH KNOW USING ONLY TEXTURES.
subtitle: Rough? Smooth? Prickly? Soft? Let them guess who.

task: WATCH FOR THE MOMENT SOMEONE STOPS LISTENING TO YOU.
subtitle: Their eyes glaze. Their nods become automatic. Then call them on it.

task: ASK THEM WHAT THEY'VE CHANGED THEIR MIND ABOUT RECENTLY.
subtitle: Not a big life thing. Something small. An opinion. A habit.

task: NAME SOMETHING THAT'S ACTUALLY GOTTEN WORSE IN RECENT YEARS.
subtitle: Not politics. Something specific and observable.

task: OBSERVE HOW SOMEONE'S FACE CHANGES WHEN THEY REMEMBER SOMETHING.
subtitle: That split-second shift. Capture it and describe it back to them.

task: ASK WHAT SMALL THING SOMEONE DID FOR THEM THAT MATTERED HUGE.
subtitle: Not a big gesture. One small act that actually meant everything.

task: TAKE TURNS NAMING PEOPLE YOU'D BE NERVOUS TO RUN INTO.
subtitle: Not dangerous people. Just awkward or complicated people.

task: COUNT THE EXACT NUMBER OF TIMES SOMEONE TOUCHES THEIR FACE.
subtitle: Two minutes. Every touch. Eyes, nose, mouth, cheeks. All of it.

task: ASK THEM WHEN THEY LAST FELT GENUINELY SURPRISED.
subtitle: Not shocked. Surprised. Delighted surprise if you're lucky.

task: DESCRIBE YOUR IDEAL EVENING USING ONLY PREPOSITIONS.
subtitle: Under a blanket. On a couch. With a person. By a fire. See?

task: SPOT THE PERSON IN THIS ROOM WHO LOOKS MOST COMFORTABLE.
subtitle: How is their body positioned? What are they not doing?

task: ASK WHAT THEY THINK YOU'RE SECRETLY BETTER AT THAN YOU ADMIT.
subtitle: They've noticed something you downplay.

task: NAME THREE HABITS YOU BOTH THINK ARE WEIRDLY COMMON.
subtitle: Things everyone does but nobody talks about.

task: WATCH SOMEONE'S HANDS WHILE THEY'RE NOT PAYING ATTENTION.
subtitle: Still? Fidgeting? Clenched? Open? Six minutes of observation.

task: ASK WHAT SKILL THEY HAVE THAT THEY NEVER GET TO USE.
subtitle: Something they're actually good at but rarely have occasion for.

task: TAKE TURNS COMPLETING THIS: NOBODY KNOWS I ACTUALLY CARE ABOUT.
highlight: NOBODY KNOWS I ACTUALLY CARE ABOUT
subtitle: Something small. Something you don't advertise.

task: COUNT HOW MANY TIMES SOMEONE SHIFTS THEIR WEIGHT IN FOUR MINUTES.
subtitle: Left foot to right foot. Standing to lean. Every shift.

task: ASK THEM WHAT THEY'RE IRRATIONALLY PROUD OF.
subtitle: Not a major accomplishment. Something small and slightly ridiculous.

task: DESCRIBE A PERSON YOU BOTH KNOW USING ONLY ANIMALS.
subtitle: Not what they look like. How they move or behave.

task: WATCH FOR THE MOMENT WHEN SOMEONE'S TONE SHIFTS DRAMATICALLY.
subtitle: Happy to serious. Calm to frustrated. Notice the exact moment.

task: ASK WHAT THEY'VE LIED ABOUT IN THE LAST WEEK.
highlight: LIED ABOUT IN THE LAST WEEK
subtitle: A white lie. A small fib. We've all told one. What was yours?

task: NAME SOMETHING THAT'S BECOME LESS COOL OVER TIME.
subtitle: Hipster culture? Skinny jeans? Specific examples required.

task: OBSERVE SOMEONE'S PUPILS FOR TWO MINUTES STRAIGHT.
subtitle: Do they dilate or contract? How much can you actually see?

task: ASK WHAT THEY WERE WEARING EXACTLY ONE MONTH AGO TODAY.
subtitle: Can they remember? Or does a whole month blur together?

task: TAKE TURNS NAMING SOMETHING YOU'D NEVER ADMIT AT A DINNER PARTY.
highlight: NEVER ADMIT AT A DINNER PARTY
subtitle: Something private. Something real.

task: COUNT HOW MANY TIMES SOMEONE BLINKS PER MINUTE EXACTLY.
subtitle: Most people blink 15–20 times per minute. They probably have no idea.

task: ASK THEM WHAT THEY THINK IS OVERBLOWN IN POPULAR CULTURE.
subtitle: A trend. A genre. A celebrity. Something everyone buys into.

task: DESCRIBE YOUR WORST DECISION USING ONLY DIALOGUE.
subtitle: What did you say? What did they say? Act out the key exchange.

task: SPOT WHO IN THIS ROOM IS MOST NERVOUS RIGHT NOW.
subtitle: Not worried. Nervous. What's the tell?

task: ASK WHAT THEY THINK THEY'RE OBVIOUSLY GOOD AT BUT INSECURE ABOUT.
subtitle: They excel at something they don't believe in themselves for.

task: NAME FIVE THINGS THAT ARE CHEAPER THAN THEY FEEL LIKE THEY SHOULD BE.
subtitle: Not objectively cheap. Things that seem underpriced.

task: WATCH SOMEONE'S NECK AREA WHILE THEY TALK FOR EXACTLY FOUR MINUTES.
subtitle: Tension? Flushing? Adam's apple movement? What can you read?

task: ASK THEM WHAT THEY'VE NEVER BEEN BRAVE ENOUGH TO TRY.
subtitle: Not skydiving. Something within reach that scares them.

task: TAKE TURNS NAMING AWKWARD THINGS PEOPLE SOMETIMES DO IN PUBLIC.
subtitle: Picking something out of teeth. Adjusting underwear. Name them.

task: COUNT HOW MANY TIMES SOMEONE SAYS 'LIKE' OR 'YOU KNOW' IN FIVE MINUTES.
subtitle: Everyone has a verbal tic. What's theirs?

task: ASK WHAT THEY THINK THEY'D BE GOOD AT IF THEY ACTUALLY TRIED.
subtitle: Something they've never pursued but suspect they could excel at.

task: DESCRIBE YOUR BIGGEST REGRET WITHOUT MENTIONING WHAT IT IS.
subtitle: Just the feeling. The timing. The aftermath. Let them guess.

task: OBSERVE SOMEONE'S EYEBROWS FOR TWO MINUTES WITHOUT THEM NOTICING.
subtitle: Do they move when they talk? Stay still? What's the baseline?

task: ASK THEM WHAT QUALITY THEY HAVE THAT THEY THINK IS UNATTRACTIVE.
subtitle: Something about themselves they view negatively that others might love.

task: NAME SOMETHING THAT'S TRENDY NOW THAT WILL BE RIDICULOUS IN FIVE YEARS.
highlight: WILL BE RIDICULOUS IN FIVE YEARS
subtitle: Fashion. Slang. Apps. Make your prediction.

task: WATCH SOMEONE'S FEET WHILE THEY'RE SITTING FOR THREE MINUTES.
subtitle: Bouncing? Still? Tucked? What's their resting position?

task: ASK WHAT THEY THINK ABOUT WHEN THEY CAN'T SLEEP.
subtitle: Not white noise thoughts. Actual thoughts. Rumination. Worries.

task: TAKE TURNS COMPLETING THIS: I PRETEND TO LIKE MORE THAN I ACTUALLY DO.
subtitle: A movie. A food. A person. Be honest.

task: COUNT HOW MANY TIMES SOMEONE CLEARS THEIR THROAT IN TEN MINUTES.
subtitle: Nervous tic? Dry mouth? Annotation of speech?

task: ASK THEM WHAT MAKES THEM FEEL MOST LIKE THEMSELVES.
subtitle: Not what they do for a living. What activity or situation?

task: DESCRIBE SOMEONE'S VOICE USING ONLY WEATHER WORDS.
subtitle: Stormy? Sunny? Foggy? Breezy? Let them figure out who.

task: SPOT THE PERSON IN THIS ROOM WITH THE MOST DISTINCTIVE WALK.
subtitle: Then imitate it for ten seconds.

task: ASK WHAT THEY THINK YOU GET WRONG ABOUT THEM.
subtitle: A assumption. A misreading. What do you consistently miss?

task: NAME SOMETHING THAT'S STILL GOOD EVEN THOUGH IT'S CLICHÉD.
subtitle: Sunset photos. A handwritten note. Some things earn their popularity.

task: OBSERVE HOW SOMEONE'S PUPILS RESPOND TO LIGHT FOR SIXTY SECONDS.
subtitle: Look for dilation and constriction. Change the light if you can.

task: ASK THEM WHAT ADVICE THEY'D GIVE TO THEIR YOUNGER SELF.
subtitle: One thing. One piece they'd go back and say.

task: TAKE TURNS NAMING THINGS THAT ARE WEIRDLY SATISFYING.
subtitle: Popping bubble wrap. Watching someone fold a fitted sheet. Specific things.

task: COUNT HOW MANY TIMES SOMEONE SCRATCHES IN SEVEN MINUTES.
subtitle: Arm. Head. Back. Leg. Every scratch is worth noting.

task: ASK WHAT THEY THINK PEOPLE UNDERESTIMATE ABOUT YOU.
subtitle: A capability. A strength. Something they see that you don't advertise.

task: DESCRIBE YOUR CHILDHOOD BEDROOM USING ONLY SHAPES.
subtitle: Round, square, triangular. See how much they can reconstruct.

task: WATCH FOR THE FIRST TIME SOMEONE MAKES A SELF-DEPRECATING JOKE.
subtitle: Notice if they laugh first or wait for your response.

task: ASK THEM WHAT SMALL THING WOULD MAKE TOMORROW SIGNIFICANTLY BETTER.
subtitle: Not a life change. One small thing. What would shift their mood?

task: NAME THREE THINGS NOBODY GETS YOU ABOUT YOURSELF.
subtitle: Misunderstandings people consistently have about who you are.

task: OBSERVE SOMEONE'S BREATHING PATTERN FOR EXACTLY THREE MINUTES.
subtitle: Fast? Slow? Shallow? Deep? Does it change when they notice you're watching?

task: ASK WHAT THEY FIND STRANGE ABOUT THEIR OWN FAMILY.
subtitle: Every family is weird in specific ways. What's the quirk?

task: TAKE TURNS NAMING THINGS THAT FEEL LIKE THEY SHOULD BE ILLEGAL.
subtitle: Not serious crimes. Petty annoyances elevated.

task: COUNT EXACTLY HOW MANY TIMES SOMEONE ADJUSTS THEIR HAIR IN SIX MINUTES.
subtitle: Tucking. Smoothing. Running fingers through. Every adjustment.

task: ASK THEM WHAT THEY'D DO DIFFERENTLY IF NOBODY WAS WATCHING.
subtitle: Not something extreme. Just... different. More real.

task: DESCRIBE YOUR PERFECT SATURDAY AFTERNOON WITHOUT USING TIME WORDS.
subtitle: No 'morning', 'hours', 'later'. Just the sequence of actions.

task: SPOT THE PERSON IN THIS ROOM WHOSE SMILE REACHES THEIR EYES MOST.
subtitle: That's the genuine one. Notice the difference from fake smiles.

task: ASK WHAT THEY THINK THEY SHOULD'VE BEEN TOLD MORE OFTEN.
subtitle: A validation. A reassurance. What did they need to hear?

task: NAME SOMETHING THAT'S MORE INTERESTING IN THEORY THAN IN PRACTICE.
subtitle: Skydiving? Travel? Career fields? Something that sounded good on paper.

task: WATCH SOMEONE'S MOUTH WHILE THEY LISTEN FOR FOUR MINUTES.
subtitle: Do they mirror your expression? Tighten? Stay neutral?

task: ASK THEM WHAT THEY LEARNED ABOUT THEMSELVES IN THE LAST MONTH.
subtitle: A realization. A pattern they noticed. Something unexpected.

task: TAKE TURNS COMPLETING THIS: I WISH MORE PEOPLE UNDERSTOOD THAT.
subtitle: Something true about the world or human nature you've noticed.

task: COUNT HOW MANY TIMES SOMEONE'S FOREHEAD CREASES IN FIVE MINUTES.
subtitle: Concentration? Worry? Every crease is data.

task: ASK WHAT THEY'D BE EMBARRASSED TO HAVE FOUND ON THEIR PHONE.
subtitle: A note. A search history. A photo. What would mortify them?

task: DESCRIBE SOMEONE YOU BOTH KNOW USING ONLY TEMPERATURES.
subtitle: Warm? Cool? Burning? Frozen? Let them guess who.

task: SPOT WHO IN THIS ROOM LOOKS MOST LIKE THEY NEED A NAP.
subtitle: What's the physical indicator? Heavy eyes? Slumped shoulders?

task: ASK THEM WHAT THEY THINK IS THEIR MOST POLARIZING TRAIT.
subtitle: Something about them that people either love or hate.

task: NAME FIVE THINGS THAT ARE WEIRDLY HARDER NOW THAN THEY USED TO BE.
subtitle: Not because you've gotten older. Because the world changed.

task: OBSERVE SOMEONE'S EYELIDS FOR TWO MINUTES EXACTLY.
subtitle: Movement pattern. Speed. How often do they blink?

task: ASK WHAT THEY NEVER WANT TO HAVE TO DO AGAIN.
highlight: NEVER WANT TO HAVE TO DO AGAIN
subtitle: A chore. A conversation. A situation. Something they'd gladly avoid.

task: TAKE TURNS NAMING YOUR FAVOURITE THINGS ABOUT EACH OTHER.
subtitle: Not compliments. Genuine favourite things. Two each minimum.

task: COUNT HOW MANY TIMES SOMEONE GLANCES AT A CLOCK IN EIGHT MINUTES.
subtitle: Time checking is a tell. What does it reveal about their state?

task: ASK THEM WHAT THEY THINK YOU SHOULD KNOW ABOUT THEM THAT YOU DON'T.
subtitle: Something they wish they'd told you sooner.

task: DESCRIBE YOUR WORST EXPERIENCE USING ONLY NEGATIVE ADJECTIVES.
subtitle: No verbs. No nouns. Just descriptive words. Let them piece it together.

task: WATCH SOMEONE'S CHEST AREA WHILE THEY TALK FOR EXACTLY THREE MINUTES.
subtitle: Rising and falling pattern. Tension signs. Openness.

task: ASK WHAT THEY THINK IS WEIRDLY OKAY TO TALK ABOUT NOW THAT WASN'T BEFORE.
subtitle: Mental health? Therapy? Money? What's more accepted now?

task: NAME THREE THINGS YOU'D CHANGE ABOUT THIS CURRENT MOMENT IF YOU COULD.
subtitle: Not life goals. Right now. This room. This second.

task: OBSERVE HOW SOMEONE'S POSTURE SHIFTS WHEN THEY GET EXCITED.
subtitle: Wait for them to talk about something they care about. Watch the change.

task: ASK THEM WHAT THEY THINK MAKES YOU LAUGH HARDEST.
subtitle: They've noticed the pattern. What type of humour gets you?

task: TAKE TURNS NAMING THINGS THAT ARE SECRETLY EXPENSIVE TO MAINTAIN.
subtitle: Cars. Pets. Relationships. What requires hidden upkeep?

task: COUNT HOW MANY TIMES SOMEONE'S EXPRESSION CHANGES SIGNIFICANTLY IN NINE MINUTES.
subtitle: Major shifts. Not micro-expressions. Full changes.

task: ASK THEM WHAT THEY'D REGRET NOT TELLING SOMEONE.
subtitle: Not something they've failed to say. Something unspoken.

task: DESCRIBE A MEMORY YOU BOTH SHARE USING ONLY COLOURS AND FEELINGS.
subtitle: No facts. No timeline. Just emotional impressions.

task: SPOT THE PERSON IN THIS ROOM WITH THE MOST RELAXED SHOULDERS.
subtitle: Then ask them what they were thinking about.

task: ASK WHAT THEY THINK YOU TAKE TOO SERIOUSLY.
subtitle: Something you worry about that they think is minor.

task: NAME SOMETHING THAT'S MORE VALUABLE NOW THAN IT WAS FIVE YEARS AGO.

subtitle: Are you the shape you think you are?

task: WALK EXACTLY THIRTY PACES IN ANY DIRECTION, THEN STOP.
subtitle: Mark where you started. Return there eventually.

task: FIND SOMETHING THAT'S BEEN MODIFIED AND GUESS WHY.
subtitle: A carved initial. A covered-up marking. What was the reason?

task: LISTEN FOR THE SOUND OF WIND AND NOTHING ELSE.
subtitle: Block out other noises mentally. When did you last hear just wind?

task: FIND A SURFACE AND MAP ITS TEMPERATURE WITH YOUR HAND.
subtitle: Which parts are warm, which are cold? Why?

task: WALK FORWARD WHILE RECITING SOMETHING YOU MEMORIZED.
subtitle: A poem, song lyrics, multiplication table. Use the walk as the metronome.

task: FIND SOMETHING THAT APPEARS DUPLICATED NEARBY.
subtitle: Two trees, two benches, two cracks. Are they ever truly identical?

task: STAND ON ONE LEG AND WATCH THE WORLD TILT.
subtitle: Three minutes. Don't touch down until time's up.

task: FIND EVIDENCE OF AN ANIMAL AND FOLLOW THE TRAIL.
subtitle: Paw prints, droppings, disturbed grass. Where does it lead?

task: PHOTOGRAPH FIVE DIFFERENT TEXTURES WITHOUT SHOWING COLOR.
subtitle: Rough, smooth, bumpy, flat. Let monochrome be the focus.

task: FIND A CORNER AND MEDITATE THERE FOR FIVE MINUTES.
subtitle: You don't need instruction. Just sit and be present.

task: WALK A FIGURE EIGHT PATTERN FOR THREE MINUTES STRAIGHT.
subtitle: Keep the loops even. This is your personal racetrack.

task: FIND SOMETHING THAT SHOULDN'T BE OUTSIDE AND QUESTION IT.
subtitle: How did it get here? How long has it been abandoned?

task: LOOK FOR ANIMALS IN CAMOUFLAGE FOR FOUR MINUTES.
subtitle: They're hiding. You might not find anything. The search is the point.

task: WALK WHILE TOUCHING EVERY SURFACE YOU PASS.
subtitle: Wood, metal, concrete, brick. Your hands are your guide.

task: FIND SOMETHING BEAUTIFUL AND SOMETHING UGLY NEARBY.
subtitle: Which is which? Are they really so different?

task: SPIN IN PLACE UNTIL YOU'VE MADE ONE FULL ROTATION.
subtitle: Take it slowly. See everything as you pass it.

task: FIND THE COLDEST SPOT YOU CAN AND STAND IN IT.
subtitle: Shade, wind, elevation. How did you know?

task: COLLECT STONES AND ARRANGE THEM IN HEIGHT ORDER.
subtitle: Precision matters. Make this arrangement deliberate.

task: WALK UNTIL YOU'VE CROSSED THREE DIFFERENT SURFACE TYPES.
subtitle: Grass to concrete to gravel. Document each transition.

task: FIND AN OPENING AND LOOK THROUGH IT TOWARD DISTANT SPACE.
subtitle: A doorway, a gap in trees. What frame do you see?

task: LISTEN FOR A SPECIFIC VOICE OR SOUND REPEATEDLY.
subtitle: A bird call, a siren, footsteps. How often does it return?

task: FIND SOMETHING ROUND AND SOMETHING ANGULAR NEARBY.
subtitle: Place them together. They create dialogue.

task: WALK WHILE ONLY LOOKING AT YOUR FEET FOR FIVE MINUTES.
subtitle: Trust your ears to guide you. Where does this walk take you?

task: FIND A PUDDLE AND STUDY ITS REFLECTION SERIOUSLY.
subtitle: What floats on top, what's reflected, what's distorted?

task: PHOTOGRAPH FIVE DIFFERENT ANGLES OF THE SAME OBJECT.
subtitle: From above, below, side, close, far. How does perspective change it?

task: FIND A NATURAL PATH AND FOLLOW IT WITHOUT VEERING.
subtitle: Worn grass, animal trail, water runoff. Let it lead.

task: STAND AND FEEL THE AIR TEMPERATURE ON YOUR SKIN.
subtitle: Warm, cool, moving, still. Become a living thermometer.

task: WALK WHILE MOVING ONLY YOUR ARMS FOR ONE MINUTE.
subtitle: Exaggerate the motion. Look ridiculous. Own it.

task: FIND SOMETHING THAT'S ROOTED AND STUDY IT DEEPLY.
subtitle: A tree, a building's foundation. What makes it stay?

task: COUNT YOUR BREATHS WHILE STANDING COMPLETELY STILL.
subtitle: Get to twenty. How long did that take? Where's the urgency?

task: FIND A COLOR THAT'S UNUSUAL IN THIS SPACE AND TRACK IT.
subtitle: Does it appear once or multiple times? Is it accidental or placed?

task: WALK IN THE LARGEST CIRCLE YOU CAN MAKE.
subtitle: Use landmarks to keep it round. Return to where you started.

task: FIND EVIDENCE OF HUMAN ACTIVITY AND DATE IT.
subtitle: How fresh? How old? What do the clues tell you?

task: HUM WHILE WALKING AND LISTEN TO HOW YOUR VOICE ECHOES.
subtitle: Does it bounce off buildings? Swallow into grass?

task: FIND SOMETHING FRAGILE AND OBSERVE IT WITHOUT DISTURBING.
subtitle: A spiderweb, a butterfly, a seedling. Respect its existence.

task: WALK WHILE TIMING YOUR STEPS TO YOUR HEARTBEAT.
subtitle: Feel your pulse. Sync your gait to your rhythm.

task: FIND SOMETHING THAT CONNECTS TWO SEPARATE THINGS.
subtitle: A bridge, a vine, a shadow. What would be missing without it?

task: LIE ON YOUR BACK AND COUNT CLOUDS FOR FIVE MINUTES.
subtitle: Count formations, not individual clouds. See the complexity.

task: FIND A SURFACE THAT'S WARM AND ONE THAT'S COLD.
subtitle: Stand on both. Feel the temperature difference.

task: WALK FORWARD WHILE EXCLUSIVELY LOOKING LEFT FOR TWO MINUTES.
subtitle: You'll veer left. That's the point. Feel your body's geometry.

task: FIND A MARK AND GUESS WHO MADE IT AND WHEN.
subtitle: A spray-painted tag, a carved initial, a footprint. Construct a story.

task: OBSERVE A BIRD FOR TWO MINUTES WITHOUT MOVING YOUR HEAD.
subtitle: Use peripheral vision. Watch for the smallest movements.

task: FIND FIVE THINGS THAT MAKE YOU THINK OF TIME.
subtitle: Shadows, decay, growth, movement, stillness. Time is everywhere.

task: WALK WHILE DESCRIBING THE GROUND ALOUD, STEP BY STEP.
subtitle: Texture, color, sound, danger level. Narrate your journey.

task: FIND SOMETHING THAT MOVED AND SOMETHING THAT'S STILL.
subtitle: Position them intentionally. Create a composition.

task: STAND STILL AND TRACK AN AIRPLANE OVERHEAD UNTIL IT VANISHES.
subtitle: From appearance to disappearance. You're part of a vast system.

task: FIND THE MOST INTERESTING SHADOW AND PHOTOGRAPH IT.
subtitle: Shape, definition, length. Light and dark as art.

task: WALK IN A RANDOM PATTERN FOR FIVE MINUTES, THEN STOP.
subtitle: Let intuition guide direction. No planning. Pure response.

task: FIND SOMETHING SOFT AND PRESS YOUR FACE AGAINST IT.
subtitle: Moss, grass, fabric, fur. Gentle sensory immersion.

task: COUNT HOW MANY DIFFERENT CREATURES YOU SEE OR HEAR.
subtitle: Species variety is a marker of health. What's the count?

task: FIND A VERTICAL LINE AND WALK ALONG IT PERFECTLY.
subtitle: A wall edge, a shadow line, a painted stripe. Don't deviate.

task: LISTEN TO YOUR FOOTSTEPS AND COUNT THEM FOR ONE MINUTE.
subtitle: The rhythm is hypnotic. Let it anchor you.

task: FIND SOMETHING THAT'S WEATHERED AND TOUCH ITS HISTORY.
subtitle: Time has written a story on this surface. Can you read it?

task: WALK WHILE SWINGING YOUR ARMS IN LARGE CIRCLES.
subtitle: Three minutes. Don't mind the looks. Feel your amplitude.

task: FIND SOMETHING THAT'S GROWN AND SOMETHING THAT'S SHRUNK.
subtitle: One expanding, one diminishing. They're in opposite conversations.

task: STAND IN ONE PLACE AND LISTEN ONLY TO DISTANT SOUNDS.
subtitle: Ignore what's near. Filter for what's far. What stories come from distance?

task: PHOTOGRAPH EVIDENCE OF SOMETHING YOU NEVER NOTICED BEFORE.
subtitle: Architectural detail, plant species, animal sign. It was always there.

task: FIND A SPOT WITH THE MOST TRAFFIC PASSING THROUGH IT.
subtitle: Sit there. Watch the flow. You're observing civilization.

task: WALK WHILE CLAPPING A RHYTHM FOR FOUR MINUTES STRAIGHT.
subtitle: Keep time. Don't let it falter. Synchronization is meditation.

task: FIND SOMETHING THAT GLOWS OR SHINES AND STUDY ITS SURFACE.
subtitle: What creates the light? Is it natural or captured?

task: WALK TOWARD THE NEAREST TREE AND TOUCH ITS BARK.
subtitle: Feel its age. How long has it been standing?

task: FIND FIVE EXAMPLES OF HUMAN GEOMETRY IN THE SPACE.
subtitle: Right angles, straight lines, regular patterns. Count them all.

task: STAND STILL AND FEEL HOW THE WIND MOVES AROUND YOU.
subtitle: Every cell of your body is an antenna. Receive the signal.

task: WALK WHILE FOCUSING ON THE SENSATION IN YOUR HANDS.
subtitle: Temperature, air movement, tension. What do they tell you?

task: FIND SOMETHING THAT'S BEEN REPAIRED AND STUDY THE SEAM.
subtitle: The break point is interesting. How was it fixed?

task: LIE DOWN AND MAKE A SNOW ANGEL WITH YOUR BODY.
subtitle: Wet ground, dirt, grass—any surface. Leave your impression.

task: FIND A SOUND THAT MAKES YOU UNCOMFORTABLE AND LOCATE IT.
subtitle: Confront what irritates you. What is it really doing?

task: WALK WHILE TOUCHING YOUR NOSE, EARS, AND MOUTH REPEATEDLY.
subtitle: Cycle through your facial features for three minutes.

task: FIND SOMETHING THAT REPRESENTS GROWTH AND DOCUMENT IT.
subtitle: New leaves, sprouting seeds, expanding branches. Life expanding.

task: STAND ON ONE LEG AND OBSERVE THE WORLD FROM THAT HEIGHT.
subtitle: Perspective shifts slightly. That shift is profound.

task: FIND A CONVERSATION HAPPENING NEARBY AND LISTEN ONLY.
subtitle: No context. Just words. What story do they tell?

task: WALK WHILE SOFTLY SINGING THE SAME SONG REPEATEDLY.
subtitle: Let it anchor your pace. The song becomes your heartbeat.

task: FIND SOMETHING THAT'S EMPTY AND LOOK INSIDE CAREFULLY.
subtitle: A can, a bottle, a shelter. What did it contain?

task: PHOTOGRAPH THE BIGGEST THING YOU CAN SEE FROM HERE.
subtitle: Building, tree, sky. Capture its dominance.

task: FIND A SURFACE COVERED IN PATTERNS AND TRACE THEM ALL.
subtitle: Brick, tile, bark. Your finger or a stick. Don't miss a line.

task: WALK WHILE EXCLUSIVELY LOOKING AT THE HORIZON.
subtitle: Where earth meets sky. Let it pull you forward.

task: FIND A SMELL THAT TRIGGERS A MEMORY AND EXPLORE IT.
subtitle: What memory? Why that one? Sit with the surprise.

task: STAND STILL AND COUNT HOW MANY TIMES A CAR PASSES.
subtitle: Three minutes of counting. What's the frequency?

task: FIND THE SMALLEST LIVING THING AND OBSERVE IT.
subtitle: An ant, a beetle, a seedling. What is it doing?

task: WALK IN A PERFECT STRAIGHT LINE FOR AS LONG AS POSSIBLE.
subtitle: Use landmarks to stay true. How far can you maintain it?

task: FIND SOMETHING THAT'S ASYMMETRICAL AND DOCUMENT BOTH SIDES.
subtitle: The imbalance is the interest. Photograph each half.

task: BREATHE DEEPLY AND SMELL EVERYTHING FOR FIVE MINUTES.
subtitle: Your nose is a vast sensor. What's your environment's signature?

task: FIND A CORNER AND OBSERVE WHAT COLLECTS THERE.
subtitle: Debris, dust, light. Why do corners gather?

task: WALK BACKWARD COUNTING ONLY ODD NUMBERS UP TO FIFTY.
subtitle: Engage your mind and body in opposite directions.

task: FIND SOMETHING THAT LOOKS UNFINISHED AND IMAGINE ITS ENDING.
subtitle: Partial structure, incomplete process. What was intended?

task: STAND IN SHADOW AND WATCH THE LIGHT CHANGE POSITION.
subtitle: The sun moves visibly if you wait. Time becomes tangible.

task: FIND A REPETITIVE SOUND AND SYNCHRONIZE YOUR WALKING TO IT.
subtitle: Clock chime, traffic signal, dripping water. Find the pulse.

task: PHOTOGRAPH YOUR SHADOW AT THREE DIFFERENT TIMES.
subtitle: How has it changed in length and darkness?

task: FIND SOMETHING THAT CHANGED COLOR AND HYPOTHESIZE WHY.
subtitle: Fading, rust, mold, sun damage. What was the original?

task: WALK WHILE BOUNCING AN IMAGINARY BALL FOR FIVE MINUTES.
subtitle: Keep consistent bounce height. Feel the rhythm.

task: FIND A PLACE WHERE YOU'RE INVISIBLE AND SIT THERE.
subtitle: Hidden in plain sight. What does being unseen feel like?

task: EXAMINE CRACKS IN A SURFACE AND MAP THEIR PATTERNS.
subtitle: They're not random. What physics created this design?

task: WALK WHILE KEEPING YOUR HANDS AT A PRECISE HEIGHT.
subtitle: Shoulder height, waist height, knee height. Choose one. Maintain it.

task: FIND SOMETHING THAT'S LEANING AND CONSIDER ITS STABILITY.
subtitle: Will it fall? How long has it been like this?

task: STAND AND LET YOUR EYES ADJUST TO SHADOWS FOR THREE MINUTES.
subtitle: Details emerge. The darkness becomes less dark.

task: FIND A PERSON FROM DISTANCE AND OBSERVE THEIR ROUTINE.
subtitle: Their actual behavior, not their appearance. What's their pattern?

task: WALK WHILE DELIBERATELY STUMBLING EVERY TEN STEPS.
subtitle: Controlled clumsiness for two minutes.

task: FIND SOMETHING THAT'S BEEN PAINTED OVER AND GUESS WHAT WAS.
subtitle: Layers of history. Can you see the edges?

task: PHOTOGRAPH A MOMENT WHEN NOBODY IS LOOKING AT YOU.
subtitle: Genuine solitude. Capture the feeling.

task: FIND AN EDGE AND FOLLOW IT FOR AS LONG AS IT EXISTS.
subtitle: Where one thing ends and another begins. Trace the boundary.

task: WALK WHILE WHISPERING NUMBERS SEQUENTIALLY FOR FIVE MINUTES.
subtitle: No one can hear you. You can hear yourself.

task: FIND SOMETHING WITH LAYERED DEPTH AND PHOTOGRAPH EACH LAYER.
subtitle: Foreground, middle, background. How do they interact?

task: STAND STILL WHILE DOCUMENTING EVERY MOVEMENT YOU NOTICE.
subtitle: Leaves, clouds, people, animals. Nothing is truly static.

task: WALK WHILE MAKING ONLY RIGHT TURNS FOR EIGHT MINUTES.
subtitle: You'll spiral inward or outward. Embrace the geometry.

task: FIND SOMETHING THAT'S BEEN DISCARDED AND QUESTION ITS STORY.
subtitle: Why was it left? What was its purpose?

task: PHOTOGRAPH THE LIGHTEST AND DARKEST THINGS YOU CAN FIND.
subtitle: Contrast is the grammar of visibility.

task: FIND A SPOT WITH PERFECT SILENCE AND MEDITATE THERE.
subtitle: Quiet enough that your thoughts become loud.

task: WALK WHILE ALTERNATING BETWEEN FAST AND SLOW PACES.
subtitle: No consistent rhythm. Follow your impulse.

task: FIND A REFLECTION OF THE SKY AND STUDY IT CAREFULLY.
subtitle: Water, metal, glass. How does it distort reality?

task: STAND IN THE CENTER OF SPACE AND DESCRIBE EVERYTHING AROUND.
subtitle: A full 360 degree inventory. What's closest, farthest, above, below?

task: FIND SOMETHING THAT'S BEEN MARKED WITH GRAFFITI OR CARVING.
subtitle: Someone left their signature here. Why?

task: WALK FORWARD WHILE MOVING YOUR HEAD SIDE TO SIDE.
subtitle: Your body goes straight but your view is scanning.

task: FIND THE QUIETEST CORNER AND LISTEN FOR TEN MINUTES.
subtitle: Silence is never truly silent. What sounds emerge?

task: PHOTOGRAPH THREE EXAMPLES OF IMPERFECTION NEARBY.
subtitle: Cracks, stains, misalignment. Flaws are features.

task: FIND A VERTICAL SURFACE AND STUDY ITS DEGRADATION PATTERN.
subtitle: Water stains, wear patterns, fading. What does time write here?

task: WALK WHILE KEEPING ONE ARM ABOVE YOUR HEAD.
subtitle: For three minutes. Feel the asymmetry.

task: FIND SOMETHING BEAUTIFUL ABOUT SOMETHING ORDINARY.
subtitle: A utility box. A puddle. A wall. Change how you see it.

task: STAND WITH YOUR EYES CLOSED AND LOCATE SOUNDS PRECISELY.
subtitle: Which direction? How far? Trust your ears.

task: FIND A PATH THAT'S WORN AND WALK IT FORWARD AND BACKWARD.
subtitle: Others have passed here. Follow their footsteps.

task: WALK WHILE TOUCHING YOUR THUMB TO EACH FINGER IN SEQUENCE.
subtitle: Both hands simultaneously. Four minutes of coordination.

task: FIND A PLACE WHERE TWO MATERIALS MEET AND EXAMINE THE SEAM.
subtitle: Concrete to grass, wood to metal. How do they coexist?

task: PHOTOGRAPH YOUR SURROUNDINGS FROM THE LOWEST VANTAGE POINT.
subtitle: Lie down. Shoot from the ground. The world looks different.

task: FIND AN OPENING AND POSITION YOURSELF TO FRAME A DISTANT VIEW.
subtitle: Use the doorway, window, or gap as a frame.

task: WALK WHILE ALTERNATING WHICH FOOT LANDS ON EVERY CRACK.
subtitle: Step on cracks intentionally. Own the superstition.

task: STAND STILL AND CATALOG EVERY TEXTURE WITHIN ARM'S REACH.
subtitle: Rough, smooth, bumpy, slick. Touch them methodically.

task: FIND A LIVING THING AND CONSIDER ITS PERSPECTIVE.
subtitle: What does the world look like from their viewpoint?

task: WALK WHILE RECITING FIBONACCI NUMBERS FOR FIVE MINUTES.
subtitle: Math as meditation. One, one, two, three, five...

task: FIND EVIDENCE OF WEATHER AND DOCUMENT EACH TYPE.
subtitle: Wind damage, water stains, sun fading. Time made visible.

task: STAND STILL AND FEEL WHERE TENSION IS IN YOUR BODY.
subtitle: Shoulders, jaw, hands. Release it one area at a time.

task: FIND THE MOST COLORFUL THING IN SIGHT AND STUDY IT.
subtitle: Saturation, combination, contrast. Why does it draw attention?

task: WALK IN A QUESTION MARK SHAPE AND CONTEMPLATE ITS MEANING.
subtitle: The path itself is inquiry.

task: FIND A SURFACE THAT REFLECTS LIGHT AND FOLLOW THE BEAM.
subtitle: Where does it land? How does it move?

task: PHOTOGRAPH FIVE THINGS THAT WERE NEVER SUPPOSED TO BE HERE.
subtitle: Trash, graffiti, structural damage. What's out of place?

task: STAND STILL UNTIL YOU ACHIEVE COMPLETE STILLNESS INSIDE.
subtitle: Your body is quiet. Now quiet your mind.

task: FIND A SOUND THAT BOTHERS YOU AND LISTEN TO IT INTENTIONALLY.
subtitle: What is it really? Does it change when you focus?

task: WALK WHILE MAKING A DIFFERENT FACIAL EXPRESSION EACH MINUTE.
subtitle: Happy, sad, angry, confused. Your face is sculpting air.

task: FIND SOMETHING THAT'S PERFECTLY STRAIGHT AND VERIFY ITS GEOMETRY.
subtitle: Use your eye or a shadow. Is it truly true?

task: PHOTOGRAPH THE OLDEST AND NEWEST THINGS YOU FIND TOGETHER.
subtitle: Time in juxtaposition.

task: FIND A PLACE THAT FEELS SAFE AND SIT THERE QUIETLY.
subtitle: What makes it safe? What are you protecting yourself from?

task: WALK BACKWARD WHILE HUMMING A LONG, CONTINUOUS NOTE.
subtitle: Don't stop for six minutes. Feel your voice stabilize.

task: FIND FIVE EXAMPLES OF SYMMETRY IN THE SPACE AROUND YOU.
subtitle: Mirror images are rarer than you'd expect.

task: STAND IN ONE SPOT AND DESCRIBE WHAT YOU FEEL PHYSICALLY.
subtitle: Temperature, texture against your skin, air movement.

task: FIND SOMETHING THAT'S BEEN RAINED ON AND STILL DRYING.
subtitle: Water stains, dark patches. Time transforms everything.

task: WALK WHILE MAKING A FIST AND RELEASING IT EVERY STEP.
subtitle: Tension, release, repeat. Build rhythm.

task: PHOTOGRAPH SOMETHING FROM THREE DIFFERENT FOCAL LENGTHS.
subtitle: Zoom in, zoom out, shoot from a new angle.

task: FIND A CORNER AND OBSERVE LIGHT HITTING IT OVER TIME.
subtitle: Shadows shift. The angle changes. Watch the transformation.

task: WALK WHILE RECITING POETRY YOU'VE MEMORIZED ALOUD.
subtitle: Poetry transforms a walk into performance.

task: FIND EVIDENCE OF ANIMAL ACTIVITY AND GUESS THE SPECIES.
subtitle: Tracks, scat, hair, damage. What visited here?

task: STAND STILL AND LET YOUR GAZE REST ON ONE OBJECT.
subtitle: Five minutes. Don't look away. See everything in that one thing.

task: FIND A PLACE WHERE WATER HAS SHAPED MATTER AND STUDY IT.
subtitle: Erosion, pooling, staining. Water is a sculptor.

task: WALK WHILE PRESSING DIFFERENT PARTS OF YOUR FEET DOWN.
subtitle: Heel, ball, toe, outer edge. Four distinct footfalls.

task: PHOTOGRAPH CONTRAST: SOMETHING VERY NEAR AND VERY FAR.
subtitle: Depth. Perspective. Scale.

task: FIND SOMETHING THAT'S BEEN MADE INTO ART AND EXAMINE IT.
subtitle: Someone saw potential in this object.

task: STAND AND LISTEN FOR ONE SPECIFIC ANIMAL SOUND.
subtitle: A bird, insect, or other creature. Isolate that sound.

task: FIND A VIEWPOINT AND PHOTOGRAPH WHAT YOU SEE FROM THERE.
subtitle: Composition, framing, story. This is your perspective.

task: WALK WHILE ONLY STEPPING ON SPECIFIC COLORS OR TEXTURES.
subtitle: Only green grass, only concrete, only shadows.

task: FIND A LOCATION WHERE YOU FEEL FULLY PRESENT.
subtitle: Time stops. Your mind quiets. Why this place?

task: PHOTOGRAPH FIVE EXAMPLES OF HOW TIME PASSES HERE.
subtitle: Growth, decay, movement, light change, wear.

task: STAND STILL AND ABSORB THE SPECIFIC ENERGY OF THIS PLACE.
subtitle: Every place has a vibe. What's this one?

task: FIND YOUR STARTING POINT AND REFLECT ON THE JOURNEY TAKEN.
subtitle: Where were you when you began? Who are you now?

task: RACE TO FIND THE MOST UNUSUAL THING WITHIN 100 METERS.
subtitle: No phones allowed for searching. First one back with something genuinely weird wins.

task: WALK IN SINGLE FILE AND GIVE DIRECTIONS BY TAPPING SHOULDERS ONLY.
subtitle: Left shoulder = turn left. Right = right. Both = stop. No talking allowed.

task: FIND FIVE THINGS THAT ARE ROUND WITHOUT USING YOUR EYES.
subtitle: Touch things, guess. No peeking. Everyone gets one guess each per object.

task: ARGUE THAT A TREE IS BETTER THAN A BUILDING FOR EXACTLY THREE MINUTES.
highlight: ARGUE THAT A TREE IS BETTER
subtitle: One person argues trees, one argues buildings. Timer running. No changing positions.

task: EACH PERSON CLOSES THEIR EYES FOR TEN SECONDS AND DESCRIBES ONE SOUND.
subtitle: No cheating by peeking. Compare notes after. Can you identify the same sources?

task: FIRST PERSON TO SPOT A BIRD IN FLIGHT GETS TO NAME IT.
subtitle: Whatever name they give sticks for the entire walk. No takesies backsies.

task: COLLECT FIVE LEAVES THAT COULD BE USED AS PAPER PLANES.
subtitle: Then actually fold and fly them. Whoever's flies the farthest wins the round.

task: TAKE TURNS DESCRIBING CLOUDS AS FAMOUS PEOPLE WITHOUT NAMING THEM.
subtitle: Others guess who. The more absurd the description, the better.

task: RACE TO TOUCH FIVE DIFFERENT TEXTURES WHILE BLINDFOLDED.
subtitle: Rough, smooth, fuzzy, cold, warm. Partner guides you to each one.

task: FIND A SHADOW THAT LOOKS EXACTLY LIKE ANOTHER OBJECT.
subtitle: A shadow that looks like a bird, a hand, a car. Make your case to the group.

task: WALK BACKWARDS TOGETHER FOR ONE FULL CITY BLOCK.
subtitle: Stay in a line. One person watches behind for obstacles. Go slow.

task: COUNT HOW MANY PEOPLE YOU SEE WEARING BLUE IN TEN MINUTES.
subtitle: Everyone counts separately, then compare. Who was most accurate?

task: HAVE A TEN-SECOND STARING CONTEST WITH A STRANGER WITHOUT LAUGHING.
subtitle: One person initiates. If they crack first, they lose. Awkward is the goal.

task: FIND THREE THINGS THAT COULD FUNCTION AS A WHISTLE.
subtitle: Test each one. Loudest or clearest sound wins the challenge.

task: RACE TO FIND SOMETHING THAT MATCHES EACH PERSON'S EYE COLOR.
subtitle: Could be natural or found. First match for each person wins.

task: TAKE A SELFIE WHERE NEITHER PERSON IS LOOKING AT THE CAMERA.
subtitle: Both looking away, at a monument, at the sky. Something interesting in the frame.

task: WALK EXACTLY LIKE EACH OTHER FOR TWO FULL MINUTES STRAIGHT.
subtitle: Same pace, same arm swing, same head tilt. Perfect synchronization.

task: FIND FIVE THINGS THAT COULD THEORETICALLY WORK AS A SEAT.
subtitle: Then actually test each one. Which is most uncomfortable?

task: DEBATE WHICH DIRECTION IS NORTH WITHOUT USING A PHONE.
subtitle: No cheating after. Make your best guess and stick to it. Mark it on the ground.

task: COLLECT FALLEN TWIGS AND BUILD THE TALLEST POSSIBLE STRUCTURE.
subtitle: No tools except your hands and gravity. See how tall it stands before toppling.

task: EACH PERSON TAKES ONE PHOTO OF THE SAME OBJECT FROM DIFFERENT ANGLES.
subtitle: Then compare. Whose angle was most creative?

task: RACE TO COLLECT SOMETHING THAT STARTS WITH EACH LETTER OF YOUR NAME.
subtitle: First person done wins. Objects can be anything you find.

task: HOP ON ONE LEG TO A SPECIFIC LANDMARK AND BACK.
subtitle: Choose a tree or building in the distance. First one there and back wins.

task: FIND SOMETHING THAT LOOKS LIKE A COMPLETELY DIFFERENT THING.
highlight: LOOKS LIKE A COMPLETELY DIFFERENT THING
subtitle: A crack that looks like a river. A stain that looks like a face. Convince them.

task: WALK IN A PERFECT CIRCLE FOR ONE FULL MINUTE.
subtitle: One person marks the starting point. How circular was it really?

task: FIND FIVE THINGS THAT WOULD MAKE GOOD PERCUSSION INSTRUMENTS.
subtitle: Test them. Create a five-note sequence together. Record it.

task: ARGUE THAT SIDEWALKS ARE UNDERRATED FOR EXACTLY FOUR MINUTES.
subtitle: Take it seriously. Persuade the other person with your points.

task: CLOSE YOUR EYES AND POINT TO WHERE YOU THINK THE SUN IS.
highlight: POINT TO WHERE YOU THINK THE SUN
subtitle: Everyone guesses. No peeking. Check who was closest.

task: COLLECT THINGS THAT COULD FIT INSIDE A SHOEBOX.
subtitle: Find as many as possible in ten minutes. Smallest or most interesting wins.

task: TAKE TURNS MAKING SHAPES WITH YOUR BODIES FOR THE OTHER TO GUESS.
subtitle: Use your whole body or just your arms. Three guesses per shape.

task: FIND THE MOST BEAUTIFUL ROCK YOU CAN POSSIBLY FIND.
subtitle: Everyone collects one. Then vote on whose is truly the most beautiful.

task: RACE TO SPOT TEN DIFFERENT TYPES OF BIRDS OR ANIMALS.
highlight: TEN DIFFERENT TYPES OF BIRDS OR ANIMALS
subtitle: Does not have to be alive. Statues count. First to ten wins.

task: WALK WITH YOUR HANDS BEHIND YOUR BACK FOR FIVE MINUTES.
subtitle: Observe without touching. What details do you notice that you normally miss?

task: COLLECT FIVE LEAVES WITH INTERESTING SHAPES OR PATTERNS.
subtitle: Press them between paper later. Most interesting leaf wins a point.

task: FIND SOMETHING THAT MAKES A UNIQUE SOUND WHEN TAPPED.
subtitle: Metal, wood, glass, plastic. Create a sound sequence with your finds.

task: DEBATE WHETHER TREES ARE BETTER THAN BUILDINGS FOR THREE MINUTES EXACTLY.
subtitle: Swap positions. Buildings advocate. No changing your mind mid-argument.

task: TAKE A PHOTO THAT INCLUDES BOTH OF YOU WITHOUT USING SELFIE MODE.
